meanings.usage: 形容对某人来说不熟悉、格格不入的事物
指来自其他国家的人或生物
用于描述非常悲伤、令人痛心的事件或情况
指从本国移居到另一个国家，强调离开的动作
be absorbed in表示专注于某事，沉迷其中
指因失去某人或某事而感到悲伤和痛苦
修饰形容词或动词，表示达到致死的程度
字面意思是道路上的里程标记，比喻人生或历史上的重要阶段
表示对某事非常有把握、确定无疑
通常指教堂或塔楼顶部的尖形结构
指将某物置于水面以下，或被水覆盖
指人工建造的用于储水的水体
指乡村的小房子，通常只有一层或两层
指收回说过的话或采取的行动，也可指缩回
指令人恐惧或痛苦的梦，也比喻可怕的经历
指快速地看一眼，强调动作的短暂性

examples.en: I stopped to let the car cool off.
examples.zh: 我停下来让汽车冷却下来。

examples.en: He became absorbed in making a new life for the two of us.
examples.zh: 他全神贯注地为我们俩创造新生活。

examples.en: I lacked for nothing, for he was both father and mother to me.
examples.zh: 我什么都不缺，因为他既当父亲又当母亲。

examples.en: I decided that I must have taken a wrong turning somewhere.
examples.zh: 我认定我一定是某个地方拐错弯了。

examples.en: The spire of the church of our village showing in the far distance.
examples.zh: 我们村庄教堂的尖顶在远处显现出来。

examples.en: There was nobody in sight to help me.
examples.zh: 看不到有任何人在场能帮我。

examples.en: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.
examples.zh: 他一直打算有一天回去，但不是为了留下来。

examples.en: He made me promise to go on my own.
As I was wondering what to do next, a man appeared.
examples.zh: 正当我在想下一步该怎么办时，一个人出现了。

examples.en: This time he pointed to the lake.

grammar.title: 过去完成时（Past Perfect Tense）
grammar.definition: 表示在过去某一时间之前已经完成的动作或状态，即"过去的过去"
grammar.usage: 当叙述过去发生的事件时，需要表示更早发生的动作或情况时使用。常与by the time, before, after等连用，或用于宾语从句中表示过去的过去。

examples.en: I had expected to be near my objective by now.
examples.zh: 我原本以为现在已经接近目的地了。

examples.en: I was only five when my father had taken me abroad.
examples.zh: 我父亲带我出国时我只有五岁。

grammar.title: 过去完成进行时（Past Perfect Continuous）
grammar.definition: 表示过去某个时间之前一直在进行的动作
grammar.usage: 强调动作的持续性和进行过程，常与for或since连用。

examples.en: He had been describing over and over again what we should see.
examples.zh: 他曾一遍又一遍地描述我们会看到什么。

grammar.title: used to 与 would 的区别
grammar.definition: used to表示过去的习惯性动作或状态，常含有与现在对比的意味；would只表示过去反复发生的动作，不表示状态
grammar.structure: used to + 动词原形 / would + 动词原形
grammar.usage: used to可以表示"曾经是"（状态）或"过去常常"（动作），而would只用于重复的动作。

examples.en: He always meant to go back one day, but not to stay.
examples.zh: 他一直打算有一天回去，但不是为了留下来。

examples.en: My father had described over and over again what we should see.
examples.zh: 我父亲曾一遍又一遍地描述我们会看到什么。

sentencePatterns.pattern: I had expected to + 动词原形, but...

original.en: I had expected to be near my objective by now, but everything still seemed alien to me.
original.zh: 我原本以为现在已经接近目的地了，但一切对我来说仍然很陌生。

imitations.en: I had expected to finish the work on time, but I ran out of time.
imitations.zh: 我原本以为能按时完成工作，但我时间不够了。

imitations.en: She had expected to meet him at the station, but he never came.
imitations.zh: 她原本以为会在车站见到他，但他根本没来。

original.en: It was not that I actually remembered anything at all. But my father had described over and over again...
original.zh: 并不是我真的记得什么。而是我父亲曾一遍又一遍地描述……

imitations.en: It was not that she didn't want to help, but she really had no time.
imitations.zh: 并不是她不想帮忙，而是她真的没有时间。

imitations.en: It was not that he was lazy, but he simply didn't know how to start.
imitations.zh: 并不是他懒惰，而是他根本不知道如何开始。

original.en: The curious thing was that the lake was not marked on the map.
original.zh: 奇怪的是那个湖在地图上并没有标注。

imitations.en: The curious thing was that he knew the answer but refused to tell.
imitations.zh: 奇怪的是他知道答案却不肯说。

imitations.en: The curious thing was that the door was open but nobody was inside.
imitations.zh: 奇怪的是门开着但里面没有人。

original.en: The village no longer existed because it had been submerged.
original.zh: 那个村庄不再存在了，因为它已经被淹没了。

imitations.en: He no longer lives in this city.
imitations.zh: 他不再住在这个城市了。

imitations.en: This factory no longer produces plastic bags.
imitations.zh: 这家工厂不再生产塑料袋了。
